coverage_evidence.label: adapter evidence boundary
coverage_evidence.summary: agentmemory is tracked for operator continuity and resume evidence, but current benchmark evidence does not prove durable lifecycle quality.

last_seen.description: #1 Persistent memory for AI coding agents based on real-world benchmarks

coverage_evidence.label: lifecycle and graph reference
coverage_evidence.summary: mem0 remains the ecosystem and entity-scoped lifecycle reference while ELF keeps deterministic evidence-bound writes.

last_seen.description: Universal memory layer for AI Agents

coverage_evidence.label: retrieval-debug baseline
coverage_evidence.summary: qmd is the strongest local retrieval-debug reference and has targeted live real-world adapter evidence.

last_seen.description: mini cli search engine for your docs, knowledge bases, meeting notes, whatever. Tracking current sota approaches while being all local

coverage_evidence.label: progressive disclosure UX reference
coverage_evidence.summary: claude-mem remains a product reference for progressive disclosure and viewer workflow, not a proven ELF replacement.

last_seen.description: Persistent Context Across Sessions for Every Agent –  Captures everything your agent does during sessions, compresses it with AI, and injects relevant context back into future sessions. Works with Claude Code, OpenClaw, Codex, Gemini, Hermes, Copilot, OpenCode + More

coverage_evidence.label: trajectory reference
coverage_evidence.summary: OpenViking informs hierarchical context trajectory while current adapter evidence remains incomplete.

last_seen.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.

coverage_evidence.label: temporal graph reference
coverage_evidence.summary: Graphiti/Zep remains the broader temporal graph workflow reference for current-versus-historical facts.

last_seen.description: Build Real-Time Knowledge Graphs for AI Agents

coverage_evidence.label: core versus archival memory reference
coverage_evidence.summary: Letta informs core memory block ergonomics while ELF keeps archival notes source-of-truth bound.

last_seen.description: Letta is the platform for building stateful agents: AI with advanced memory that can learn and self-improve over time.

coverage_evidence.label: research gate
coverage_evidence.summary: LightRAG is a D0 watch item with a research gate; no adapter strength claim is allowed yet.

coverage_evidence.label: research gate
coverage_evidence.summary: GraphRAG is a D0 watch item with a research gate; no adapter strength claim is allowed yet.

last_seen.description: A modular graph-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system

coverage_evidence.label: research gate
coverage_evidence.summary: RAGFlow is a D0 watch item with a research gate; no adapter strength claim is allowed yet.

last_seen.description: RAGFlow is a leading open-source Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) engine that fuses cutting-edge RAG with Agent capabilities to create a superior context layer for LLMs

coverage_evidence.label: markdown-first reference
coverage_evidence.summary: memsearch remains a source-transparency reference while current adapter evidence is incomplete or wrong-result typed.

last_seen.description: A persistent, unified memory layer for all your AI agents (e.g. Claude Code, Codex), backed by Markdown and Milvus.

coverage_evidence.label: replay regression reference
coverage_evidence.summary: LangGraph informs replay and checkpoint regression workflows; ELF traces do not replace full agent-state replay.

coverage_evidence.label: typed graph ergonomics reference
coverage_evidence.summary: nanograph is a typed graph DX reference, not a full memory backend benchmark claim.

last_seen.description: On-device property graph database. Schema-as-code. One CLI → One Folder. No Server. Think: DuckDB for graphs.

coverage_evidence.label: derived knowledge pages reference
coverage_evidence.summary: llm-wiki informs rebuildable cited knowledge pages and lint/repair loops.

last_seen.description: LLM-compiled knowledge bases for any AI agent. Parallel multi-agent research, thesis-driven investigation, source ingestion, wiki compilation, querying, and artifact generation.

coverage_evidence.label: operational brain reference
coverage_evidence.summary: gbrain informs current-truth and timeline presentation while ELF source notes remain authoritative.

coverage_evidence.label: graph-compressed navigation reference
coverage_evidence.summary: graphify informs rebuildable graph reports and pre-search guidance without replacing ELF storage.

last_seen.description: AI coding assistant skill (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Cursor, Gemini CLI, and more). Turn any folder of code, SQL schemas, R scripts, shell scripts, docs, papers, images, or videos into a queryable knowledge graph. App code + database schema + infrastructure in one graph.
